body: PLACEHOLDER (directional, not a licensed feed): Lower-middle-market field-services businesses continue to attract consolidator interest, with recurring-contract mix and route density the two attributes buyers reward most.
PLACEHOLDER precedent note: A regional facility-services provider in the $3–5M EBITDA band reportedly transacted around the mid-5x LTM EBITDA range, in line with the placeholder sector median.

PLACEHOLDER (directional, not a licensed feed): Precision-manufacturing sellers with documented quality systems and diversified end-markets tend to clear at the upper end of the placeholder spread; single-customer concentration compresses it.
PLACEHOLDER precedent note: A contract manufacturer in the $3–5M EBITDA band was described as trading near 5.7x LTM EBITDA, consistent with the placeholder sector median for its size.

PLACEHOLDER (directional, not a licensed feed): Distribution multiples hinge on working-capital intensity and supplier terms; asset-light, high-turn operators command a premium to the placeholder median.
PLACEHOLDER precedent note: A specialty wholesale distributor in the $1–3M EBITDA band reportedly closed in the mid-4x LTM EBITDA range, near the placeholder sector median.

PLACEHOLDER (directional, not a licensed feed): Healthcare-services platforms with credentialed providers and payer diversification anchor the top of the placeholder range; reimbursement exposure is the key discount factor.
PLACEHOLDER precedent note: A multi-site behavioral-health group in the $3–5M EBITDA band was cited near 6.7x LTM EBITDA, matching the placeholder sector median for its band.

PLACEHOLDER (directional, not a licensed feed): Software sellers are valued on net revenue retention and gross margin far more than raw growth; durable recurring revenue drives the placeholder premium over the other sectors.
PLACEHOLDER precedent note: A vertical-SaaS business in the $3–5M EBITDA band reportedly transacted around 9x LTM EBITDA, consistent with the placeholder sector median.

PLACEHOLDER (directional, not a licensed feed): Absent a sector-specific comp set, lower-middle-market businesses tend to cluster around a mid-single-digit LTM EBITDA multiple, adjusted for owner dependence and customer concentration.
PLACEHOLDER precedent note: A diversified small business in the $1–3M EBITDA band was described near the mid-4x LTM EBITDA range, in line with the placeholder default median.
